context_history.content: I want to learn about: html. DO NOT greet me. Immediately start lesson Step 1 by explaining the core concept on the whiteboard.

HTML, or HyperText Markup Language, is the standard language used to create the structure of web pages. It acts as a skeleton, using 'tags' to identify different types of content like headings, paragraphs, and links.

visual_content.detail: HTML, Structure, Webpage, HTML provides the underlying structure for every webpage.

context_history.content:
HTML uses specific tags to define content types:

creates the largest heading for titles, while

marks a paragraph of text.

steps:
This is a paragraph.

- Used for blocks of text.

context_history.content:
Beyond simple text, we use the tag to create hyperlinks that connect different web pages and the tag to embed visual images. The
tag acts as a generic container to group these elements together for layout purposes.
can you give it a code for any web page
Every HTML document follows a standard structure that begins with a declaration to tell the browser it is an HTML5 file. This structure acts as a container for all the content you want to display.

steps: <html> - The root element of the page

context_history.content: can you explain a court of Amazon web page

A complex site like Amazon is organized into major semantic sections: a <header> for the search bar and logo, a <main> area for product listings, and a <footer> for legal links. This hierarchy tells the browser exactly which part of the page serves which purpose.

visual_content.detail: Header (Search/Nav), Main (Products), Footer (Links), Amazon Layout Structure

context_history.content: HTML elements can also have 'attributes' which provide additional information or instructions to the browser. For example, the <a> tag uses the 'href' attribute to tell the browser exactly which URL to navigate to.
